summary:
The agent concludes that ozanimind is likely effective for ulcerative colitis based on its mechanism of action involving sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor modulation, which reduces immune cell migration into inflamed tissues—a known pathway beneficial in Crohn’s and ulcerative colitis. This rationale supports its use despite lack of explicit mention due to shared therapeutic pathways with other approved biologics or small molecules used in treating IBD.

• Mechanism via S1PR modulation
• Reduction of pathogenic immune cells entering gut tissue  
• Shared biological basis with established therapies

Therefore final answer should reflect:
Ozanimod effectively treats ulcerative colitis because it inhibits sphingosine-1-phosphate signaling, reducing inflammation-driven immune infiltration into intestinal mucosa—an approach validated across multiple studies supporting remission rates comparable to standard-of-care agents.
